places.name: BLR → HND/NRT
places.type: ~9–10 hr flight. Narita is well-connected to central Tokyo via the Narita Express (NEX).

places.name: Agora Place Asakusa
places.type: Hotel in the heart of old Tokyo — steps from Sensō-ji and the Sumida River. Great value with a rooftop view.

places.name: Hotel SUI Kyoto Kiyomizu
places.type: Boutique stay near Kiyomizudera with a refined Japanese aesthetic. Walking distance to Higashiyama's cobbled lanes.

places.name: Sakura Fleur Aoyama Hotel
places.type: Chic, design-forward hotel in upscale Aoyama — perfect base for your final Tokyo night before flying home.

tip.label: Tip
tip.text: Pre Book your flights and hotels in 2-3 months in advance to get the best deals.

places.name: Sumida River Walk
places.type: A peaceful riverside promenade lined with cherry trees — stunning at dusk with Tokyo Skytree looming overhead.

places.name: Don Quijote Asakusa
places.type: Japan's iconic discount wonderland open 24/7 — crams snacks, electronics, costumes, and cheap souvenirs into every inch.

tip.label: Tip
tip.text: Don Quijote (Donki) is open 24 hrs — perfect for picking up essentials, snacks & souvenirs after a long flight.

places.name: Ueno Park
places.type: Tokyo's most beloved cherry blossom spot — 1,000+ sakura trees create a dreamy pink canopy in early April.

places.name: Samurai Restaurant
places.type: An immersive dinner-and-show experience blending samurai battles, taiko drums, and theatrical Japanese performance.

places.name: Shinjuku Gyoen
places.type: A serene 58-hectare national garden fusing French, English, and Japanese garden styles — a rare calm oasis in chaotic Shinjuku.

places.name: teamLab Borderless
places.type: A mind-bending labyrinth of 60+ immersive digital artworks — light, sound, and water blur the line between art and reality.

places.name: Tokyo Tower
places.type: Tokyo's iconic 333m red-and-white lattice tower with panoramic night views — especially magical illuminated after dark.

places.name: Yodobashi Akiba
places.type: A 9-floor electronics megastore in Akihabara — the best place in the world to geek out on cameras, gadgets, and gaming gear.

tip.label: Must-do
tip.text: Book teamLab Borderless tickets in advance — they sell out weeks ahead, especially in April.

places.name: Sensō-ji Temple
places.type: Tokyo's oldest and most visited temple, dating to 645 AD — the Kaminarimon gate and Nakamise shopping street are unforgettable.

places.name: Nezu Shrine Azalea
places.type: A hidden gem with over 3,000 azalea bushes blazing in vivid pink and red — one of Tokyo's best-kept seasonal secrets.

places.name: Mario Kart Asakusa
places.type: Race through Tokyo's real streets in full Mario costume — the ultimate surreal, bucket-list Tokyo experience.

places.name: Kappabashi Town
places.type: A unique street packed with professional kitchen supply shops and hyper-realistic plastic food displays — a chef's paradise.

places.name: Shibuya Sky
places.type: An open-air rooftop observation deck atop Shibuya Scramble Square with jaw-dropping 360° views of the Tokyo skyline.

places.name: Shibuya Crossing
places.type: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing — up to 3,000 people surge across simultaneously. Stand in the middle and soak it in.

tip.label: Tip
tip.text: Go to Sensō-ji early (before 8 AM) to beat the tourist rush — the atmosphere at dawn is magical.

places.name: Hakone Shrine
places.type: 10:00 AM · 40 min · Founded in 757 AD on the shores of Lake Ashi — famous for its vermilion torii gates and a classic photo backdrop of Mt Fuji.

places.name: Heiwa no Torii
places.type: 10:40 AM · 20 min · Hakone Shrine's iconic torii gate standing in the waters of Lake Ashi — Mt Fuji reflected behind it makes for one of Japan's most breathtaking scenes.

places.name: Hakone Pirate Ship · Tōgendai Port
places.type: 11:10 AM · 30 min · Sail Lake Ashi on a replica 18th-century European warship with stunning views of Mt Fuji and the shrine torii gate. One-way ticket ~¥1,700.

places.name: Owakudani Ropeway
places.type: 11:40 AM · 30 min · Ride the Hakone Ropeway over a steaming volcanic valley — on clear days you get sweeping views of Mt Fuji and Lake Ashi far below.

places.name: Owakudani Volcanic Site
places.type: 12:10 PM · 40 min · A dramatic active volcanic zone formed 3,000 years ago — sulfurous steam vents, boiling hot springs, and the famous black eggs cooked in the geothermal pools.

places.name: Yamanakako
places.type: 1:50 PM · The largest and highest of the Fuji Five Lakes — famous for its "Upside-Down Fuji" reflection, resident swans, and the rare summer Diamond Fuji phenomenon.

places.name: Oshino Hakkai
places.type: 2:30 PM · 40 min · Eight crystal-clear spring ponds fed by Mt Fuji snowmelt filtered over decades — dubbed "Japan's Jiuzhaigou" with water so pure you can drink it straight.

places.name: Tokyo Metropolitan Gov.
places.type: Twin-towered government building with a free observation deck on the 45th floor — spectacular city views without spending a yen.

places.name: Omoide Yokocho
places.type: A narrow alley of tiny smoky yakitori bars in Shinjuku — one of Tokyo's most atmospheric places to eat skewers and sip sake.

tip.label: Tip
tip.text: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building observation deck is completely free and gives a stunning 360° view of the city at night.

places.name: Shinkansen to Kyoto
places.type: The bullet train covers Tokyo–Kyoto in about 2.5 hrs — smooth, punctual, and a thrilling ride past Mt Fuji on a clear day.

places.name: Otagi Nenbutsuji
places.type: A remote mountain temple with 1,200 moss-covered stone Rakan statues, each with a unique quirky expression — deeply atmospheric.

places.name: Arashiyama Bamboo
places.type: A cathedral of towering green bamboo stalks that creak and sway above you — one of the most iconic sights in all of Japan.

places.name: Adashino Nenbutsuji
places.type: A serene temple holding over 8,000 stone memorials for forgotten souls — hauntingly beautiful and almost always crowd-free.

places.name: Saga-toriimoto St.
places.type: One of Kyoto's best-preserved historic streets with traditional machiya townhouses, thatched roofs, and a complete absence of modernity.

tip.label: Heads up
tip.text: Otagi Nenbutsuji is closed on Wednesdays — plan accordingly if you swap days around.

places.name: Ine
places.type: 1 hr · A magical fishing village of 230+ funaya boathouses built directly over the sea — one of Japan's most scenic and unspoiled coastal spots.

places.name: Ine Bay Sightseeing Boat · Re:ine
places.type: Optional · Extra fee · Cruise Ine Bay by boat to see the funaya from the water — the best angle to appreciate this utterly unique floating village.

places.name: Amanohashidate
places.type: 2 hrs · A 3.6km pine-forested sandbar across a serene lagoon — one of Japan's three most celebrated views, best seen from the hills above.

places.name: Amanohashidate Chairlift & Monorail
places.type: Extra fee · Ride up to the viewpoint above the sandbar — the chairlift offers a breezy open-air ascent with sweeping lagoon panoramas below.

places.name: Amanohashidate Viewland
places.type: The hilltop vantage point for the classic "stock dive" view — bend over and look through your legs to see the sandbar soar like a dragon through the sky.

places.name: Kaisen Bridge
places.type: A rotating bridge at the southern tip of the sandbar that swings open to let boats through — a lovely photo stop at the start of the pine-lined walk.

places.name: Chionji Temple
places.type: A 1,300-year-old temple at the foot of the sandbar known for wisdom-granting amulets — its pagoda and tranquil grounds make for great photos.

places.name: Miyama Kayabuki-no-Sato
places.type: 40 min · A beautifully preserved thatched-roof village hidden deep in the Kyoto mountains — feels like stepping into rural Japan from centuries past.

tip.label: Must-do
tip.text: The classic view is bending over and looking at Amanohashidate through your legs — it looks like a dragon flying through the sky. Don't skip it!

sections.title: Fushimi Inari · Nara · Maruyama Park
sections.meta: Day 7 · Best day of the trip

places.name: Fushimi Inari
places.type: A mountain trail lined with thousands of vermillion torii gates donated by businesses — mystical at dawn and almost deserted before 8 AM.

places.name: Kintetsu to Nara
places.type: A quick ~35-min train from Kyoto drops you into Japan's ancient first capital, a UNESCO-listed city of temples and wild deer.

places.name: Nara Park
places.type: Over 1,300 wild sika deer roam freely here, considered sacred messengers of the gods — they'll bow for a shika senbei cracker.

places.name: Tōdai-ji
places.type: Home to the world's largest bronze Buddha statue at 15 meters tall — the wooden temple hall housing it is itself the world's largest wooden structure.

places.name: Kasugataisha Shrine
places.type: A UNESCO World Heritage shrine founded in 768 AD, with 3,000 bronze and stone lanterns lining its forested approach.

places.name: Nakatanidou
places.type: Famous for mochi pounded at lightning speed right in front of you — the chewy, fresh-made treats sell out fast and the performance is unmissable.

tip.label: Tip
tip.text: Go to Fushimi Inari at 7:30 AM — the gates are dramatic at dawn and you'll practically have the lower path to yourself. The deer at Nara Park will eat your map. Hide everything.

places.name: Yasaka Shrine
places.type: An elegant Shinto shrine glowing with lanterns at dawn — the spiritual gateway into Higashiyama's historic district and one of Kyoto's most beloved landmarks.

places.name: Ninenzaka
places.type: A beautifully preserved 700-year-old stone-paved lane lined with traditional teahouses, pottery shops, and matcha cafés — Kyoto at its most photogenic.

places.name: Sannenzaka
places.type: Ninenzaka's sister slope — equally charming and slightly steeper. Legend says stumbling here brings three years of bad luck, so tread carefully!

places.name: Kiyomizu-dera
places.type: A 1,200-year-old clifftop temple built without a single nail — its wooden stage juts dramatically over a forested hillside with sweeping views of Kyoto below.

places.name: Matcha Experience
places.type: Sit down for a traditional Japanese tea ceremony in a historic Higashiyama machiya — learn to whisk your own bowl of frothy ceremonial matcha.

places.name: Kimono Experience
places.type: Transform into a vision of old Kyoto — choose your fabric, obi, and accessories with expert staff help for a fully authentic kimono dressing.

places.name: Kimono Photoshoot
places.type: Stroll the cobblestones of Ninenzaka and Sannenzaka in full kimono for a guided photoshoot — every stone-lantern alley makes a perfect frame.

places.name: Shinkansen to Tokyo
places.type: The afternoon Shinkansen gets you back to Tokyo in ~2.5 hrs — grab an ekiben (station bento) from Kyoto Station for the ride.

tip.label: Tip
tip.text: Kiyomizudera at 7 AM is misty, quiet, and looks like a painting — completely different from how it looks in tourist photos taken midday.

places.name: Chiba
places.type: A relaxed city just east of Tokyo on Tokyo Bay — worth a morning wander through Chiba Castle or the lively Chiba Central market.

places.name: Narita Airport
places.type: Japan's main international hub — allow at least 90 min travel time from Tokyo and arrive 3 hrs early for international departures.

tip.label: Tip
tip.text: Narita is far from central Tokyo — allow 90 minutes from the city. The Narita Express (NEX) is the easiest option from Shinjuku or Shibuya.
